refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication quality control and teleoperation tasking.
Dependency on network link quality and safety alert propagation.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality control, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Risk of incomplete error correction under extreme network degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating fallback mechanisms or redundant channels if adaptive bitrate alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel for telemetry and control data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends vehicle status and communication quality metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains multiple functions beyond teleoperation, leading to higher diffusion.
Primarily physical control; less suitable for communication quality management.
High containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication.
Focus on safety alerts; unrelated to communication quality control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality control, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if communication quality impacts safety alerts.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate interface definitions with Safety Services to manage cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive bitrate and error correction) directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services for potential safety-critical communication dependencies.
Consider fallback communication strategies in DP refinement.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate algorithms can meet latency and packet loss requirements.
open_risks: Potential latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
evidence_needed: Performance data of adaptive bitrate and error correction under expected network conditions.
Interface validation between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
